name: Via Verde del Tarazona – Circular Route
name_original: Vía Verde del Tarazona – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tudela offers diverse landscapes, from the semi-desert terrain of Bardenas Reales Natural Park to the lush riverine environments along the Ebro River. The region features well-developed cycling infrastructure, including dedicated greenways and canal routes. Cyclists can explore unique geological formations, flat paths along disused railway lines, and scenic routes through agricultural areas. This variety provides a wide array of routes suitable for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Tudela The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde del Tarazona – Circular Route, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This route follows a former railway line, offering a generally flat and accessible ride. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vera Cruz Trail (The Templars' Way), a moderate 31.9 miles (51.4 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, connecting historical points of interest. Local touring cyclists also love the Tarazona – Tarazonica Greenway loop from Tudela de Navarra, a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail leading through the pleasant greenway environment, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling around Tudela is defined by semi-desert landscapes, green river corridors, and flat greenways along disused railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy canal paths to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes. The routes in Tudela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tudela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tudela?
A: There are over 90 touring cycling routes around Tudela, offering a wide variety of experiences for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 700 times.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Tudela?
A: The routes around Tudela cater to various abilities. You'll find a few easy routes, 62 moderate routes, and 25 more difficult options, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Tudela?
A: Tudela offers a fascinating contrast of landscapes. You can cycle through the unique semi-desert terrain of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, with its moon-like geological formations, or enjoy the lush riverine environments along the Ebro River and its green corridor. There are also flat routes along disused railway lines and canals, passing through agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tudela?
A: Yes, Tudela is home to several family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969208035/via-verde-del-tarazona-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Tarazona – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, following a former railway line that offers a generally flat and accessible ride. Canal routes also provide gentle, flat paths suitable for all ages.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Tudela?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Tudela are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969208035/via-verde-del-tarazona-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Tarazona – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona – Tarazonica Greenway loop from Tudela de Navarra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can encounter a mix of natural wonders and historical sites. In the Bardenas Reales, you'll find unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Cortinillas&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969284719/vera-cruz-trail-the-templars-way" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vera Cruz Trail (The Templars' Way)&lt;/a&gt; connects historical points of interest. You might also pass by serene spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3767602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Lord&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortinas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenging rides, there are routes with notable elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Nature Campsite – Great point of view loop from Universidad Pública de Navarra - Campus de Tudela&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult and features over 460 meters of ascent, offering a rewarding workout and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tudela?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for touring cycling in Tudela, offering pleasant temperatures. During summer, especially when exploring areas like the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, it's crucial to be mindful of the heat and carry sufficient water.

Q: Can I find routes that follow rivers or canals?
A: Yes, the region is rich with routes along waterways. The Ebro River provides a green corridor with dedicated cycle lanes. Additionally, several flat routes extend along various canals, such as the Imperial de Aragón, Tauste, and Lodosa canals, passing through charming towns and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Tudela?
A: The touring cycling experience in Tudela is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic semi-desert to the peaceful greenways, and the well-developed infrastructure that caters to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes provide excellent opportunities for scenic views. The Bardenas Reales Natural Park, in particular, offers dramatic vistas of its unique geological formations, such as from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Cortinillas&lt;/a&gt;. The medieval Ebro Bridge also provides panoramic views of Tudela and its natural surroundings.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Tudela offers several longer routes for extended touring. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona – Tarazonica Greenway loop from Tudela de Navarra&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 59 km, providing a substantial ride through pleasant greenway environments.
tips: Thanks to the newly built bridge, you can skilfully cross the Carretera de Ablitas without coming into contact with road traffic. There is also a small rest area for a quick break. A small memorial in the middle of the path commemorates the railway past of this route.
In spring and early summer, it is a pleasure to pedal along the road. The bright colors of the plants are a delight. You can also hear the noise of countless storks throughout the fields.

Not far from the 2315 meter high Sierra de Moncayo is the small town of Tarazona. There is a lot to discover here. In addition to the three-story town hall, which is housed in an imposing Renaissance building with balconies and a loggia on the upper floor, the cathedral, which was built in the 12th century in the French Gothic style, is also worth a visit.
